tactics.name: Emotional Flooding
tactics.description: Parent overwhelms conversation with emotional intensity, volume, repetition, and nonstop talking.
examplePhrases: This is unbelievable!
Nobody listens!
I’m sick of this school!
tactics.recommendedLeadershipResponse: Umbrella method; allow venting without absorbing emotion

tactics.name: Deflection
tactics.description: Parent shifts attention away from child behavior onto other factors.
examplePhrases: What about the other kid?
What did the teacher do first?
tactics.leadershipRisk: Leader loses focus on central issue

tactics.name: Character Attack
tactics.description: Parent attacks teacher or administrator competence/personality.
examplePhrases: Maybe this teacher shouldn’t work with kids.
tactics.recommendedLeadershipResponse: Maintain professionalism and redirect to issue

tactics.name: Forced Alliance
tactics.description: Parent attempts to emotionally recruit educator to their side.
examplePhrases: You know how boys are.
If this was your kid…

tactics.name: Victim Expansion
tactics.description: Parent expands one issue into a larger story of ongoing mistreatment.
examplePhrases: This always happens to my child.
tactics.leadershipRisk: Leader gets dragged into endless history

tactics.name: Policy Challenge
tactics.description: Parent attacks rules, policy, or fairness of procedures.
examplePhrases: Where exactly is that written?

tactics.name: District Threat
tactics.description: Parent threatens to contact district, board, media, or superintendent.
examplePhrases: I’ll call downtown tomorrow.

tactics.name: Guilt Projection
tactics.description: Parent implies educator is emotionally harming child.
examplePhrases: You have no idea what this did to her.

tactics.name: Minimization
tactics.description: Parent downplays seriousness of child behavior.
examplePhrases: It was just a joke.
Kids mess around.
tactics.notes: Common with Cool Parent or Dealer

tactics.name: Public Pressure
tactics.description: Parent threatens social exposure or public embarrassment.
examplePhrases: I’ll post this everywhere.

tactics.name: Historical Stacking
tactics.description: Parent piles unrelated past incidents into current discussion.
examplePhrases: And last year this happened too…

tactics.name: Emotional Pivot
tactics.description: Parent rapidly shifts from anger to friendliness to sadness.

tactics.name: Silence Pressure
tactics.description: Parent uses long silence to create discomfort or force talking.

tactics.name: False Resolution
tactics.description: Parent suddenly sounds calm or agreeable without true resolution.
examplePhrases: Okay fine, whatever.

tactics.name: Personalization
tactics.description: Parent shifts issue from situation to educator personally.
examplePhrases: You clearly don’t care about kids.
tactics.recommendedLeadershipResponse: Do not personalize; return to issue

tactics.name: Credential Leverage
tactics.description: Parent uses professional identity or experience to strengthen their argument.
examplePhrases: I’m a teacher too and I would NEVER do that.
I work with kids every day.
tactics.notes: Common with educator or professional parents

tactics.name: Personal Attack
tactics.description: Parent attacks the educator personally rather than discussing the issue.
examplePhrases: You clearly don’t care about children.
Maybe you’re in the wrong profession.
tactics.emotionalPurpose: Create emotional reaction and destabilize authority
tactics.recommendedLeadershipResponse: Do not defend identity; calmly redirect to issue

tactics.name: Authority Escalation
tactics.description: Parent threatens to bypass the educator and contact higher authority.
examplePhrases: I’m calling your boss.
I’ll take this to the superintendent.
tactics.emotionalPurpose: Create fear, urgency, and loss of control
tactics.leadershipRisk: Leader becomes defensive, fearful, or overly accommodating
tactics.recommendedLeadershipResponse: Remain calm, non-defensive, and transparent about process
tactics.notes: Common leverage tactic when parent feels blocked

tactics.name: Child-as-Witness
tactics.description: Parent places child into the emotional center of the conversation to increase pressure.
examplePhrases: My daughter is sitting here crying right now.
Do you want to tell him yourself why he’s suspended?
tactics.emotionalPurpose: Create guilt, urgency, and emotional discomfort
tactics.leadershipRisk: Leader softens structure out of discomfort
tactics.recommendedLeadershipResponse: Acknowledge emotion while maintaining adult-to-adult structure

tactics.name: Rapid-Fire Interrogation
tactics.description: Parent fires nonstop questions without allowing full responses.
examplePhrases: Who said that? When did this happen? Why wasn’t I called? Who approved this?
tactics.emotionalPurpose: Overwhelm leader cognition and gain conversational control
tactics.leadershipRisk: Leader over-explains, rushes, or contradicts themselves
tactics.recommendedLeadershipResponse: Slow pacing, answer selectively, maintain conversational structure
tactics.notes: Common in high-anxiety or high-control parents
